Student Supplemental Materials

Source Code

The source code for each program featured in this Revel course.

Errata




















































900
Glossary Terms


A

accessor method
A method that gets a value from a class’s attribute but does not change it.

accumulator
A variable that accumulates the total of a group of values as a loop iterates.

aggregate function
An SQL function that performs a calculation on a set of values in a database table.

algorithm
A set of well-defined logical steps that must be taken to perform a task.

alignment designator
A code in an f-string that indicates the alignment of a value withing a field.

American Standard Code for Information Interchange
A set of 128 numeric codes that represent the English letters, various punctuation marks, and other characters.

append mode
A mode for opening a file that causes all data written to the file to be appended to the end of the file's current contents.

application software
Programs that make a computer useful for everyday tasks.

argument
A piece of data that is passed into a function.

argument
A value that is sent to a function when the function is called.

ASCII
The American Standard Code for Information Interchange. It is a set of 128 numeric codes that represent the English letters, various punctuation marks, and other characters.

assembler
A program that translates an assembly language program to a machine language program.

assembly language
A low-level programming language that uses short words known as mnemonics instead of binary instructions.

assignment expression
An expression that assigns a value to a variable and returns the value that was assigned.

assignment operator
The assignment operator (=) assigns the value on its right to the variable on its left.

assignment statement
A statement that assigns a value to a variable. In Python, an assignment statement can also be used to create a variable that does not exist.

augmented assignment operator An assignment operator that is combined with a math operator.

autoincremented
An integer value that is generated by the database management system to store in an identity column. The value is 1 greater than the largest value currently stored in the identity column.


B

base case
In a recursively solved problem, the case in which the problem can be solved without recursion.

base class
The general class in an inheritance relationship.

binary digit
A bit, which is a fundamental unit of memory storage that may be turned "on" or "off." A bit can represent the values 0 or 1.

binary file
A file containing data that has not been converted to text.

binary numbering system
A numbering system in which all numeric values are written as sequences of 0s and 1s.

bit
A fundamental unit of memory storage that may be turned "on" or "off." A bit can represent the values 0 or 1.

black box
Any mechanism that accepts input, performs some operation that cannot be seen on the input, and produces output.

block
A set of statements that belong together as a group.

body of a loop
The statements that are repeated by a loop.

Boolean expression
An expression that can be evaluated as either true or false.



901
Boolean function
A function that returns either true or false.

buffer
A small "holding section" in memory.

Button
A widget that the user can click to cause an action to take place.

bytes
A unit of memory that is large enough to store a letter of the alphabet or a small number.

C

call
To execute a function.

callback function
A function that executes when a specific event takes place.

calling a function
To execute a function.

Canvas
A widget that appears as a blank rectangular area. Two-dimensional shapes can be drawn on a Canvas.

case-insensitive comparison
When strings are compared without considering the case of the letters in the strings.

central processing unit
The part of a computer that executes programs.

check button or check box
A widget that appears as a small box that can be checked or unchecked.

class
Code that specifies the attributes and methods of a particular type of object.

class definition
Code that specifies the attributes and methods of a particular type of object.

code
The statements that a programmer writes in a programming language.

code reuse
Writing the code once to perform a task, and then reusing that code each time you need to perform the task.

column
An individual piece of data about an item.

comma separated values
A file format in which the data items are separated by commas.

command line interface
An interface that typically displays a prompt and executes commands that the user types.

Comment
A note of explanation placed in a program's code, intended for the human reader of the code.

compiler
A program that translates a high-level language program into a separate machine language program.

component
A physical device that is part of a computer system.

composite key
A key that is the combination of two or more columns.

concatenation
Appending one string to the end of another.

concatenation
Appending one string to the end of another string.


condition-controlled loop A loop that uses a true/false
condition to control the number of times that it repeats.

conditional expression
A shorthand technique for writing a simple if-elsestatement.

conditionally executed
A statement or group of statements that are performed only when a certain condition is true.

constraint
A rule enforced by a database management system to limit the type of data that can be stored in a column.

control structure
A logical design that controls the order in which a set of statements execute.

cookie
A small file that contains information about a browsing session.

count-controlled loop
A loop that repeats a specific number of times.

counter variable
A variable that keeps a count of the number of iterations performed by a loop.

CPU
central processing unit

CRUD
An acronym that stands for Create, Read, Update, and Delete. These are the four basic operations performed by a database application.

CSV
comma separated values

customer
The person, group, or organization asking you to write a program.





902   Glossary
D

data attribute
A variable that is stored in an object.

data hiding
An object’s ability to hide its data from code that is outside the object.

data type
The type of data that a variable will reference.

database
A collection of data that is organized into tables, rows, and columns, and maintained by a database management system.

database management system Software that is specifically designed to store, retrieve, and manipulate large amounts of data in an organized and eficient manner.

debug
The process of finding and correcting errors in a program.

decision structure
A control structure that can execute a set of statements only under certain circumstances.

delimiter
A character that separates the tokens in a string.

depth of recursion
The number of times that a recursive function calls itself.

derived class
The specialized class in an inheritance relationship.

dialog box
A graphical window that appears on the screen for the purpose of displaying information or gathering user input.

dialog box
A small graphical window that displays information and allow the user to perform actions.

dictionary
An object that stores a collection of key-value pairs.

dictionary comprehension
A concise expression that reads the elements of one dictionary and creates another dictionary.

difference
The difference of two sets is the elements that are in one set but not the other.

digital
Any piece of data that is represented as binary numbers.

Digital data
Data that is stored in binary format.

digital device
A device that works with binary data.

direct access file
A file that allows the program to jump directly to a specific location within the file.

direct recursion
When a recursive function directly calls itself.

disk drive
A storage device that stores data by magnetically encoding it onto a circular disk.

divide and conquer
The process of dividing a large task into several smaller tasks that are easily performed.

dual alternative decision structure A decision structure that has two possible paths of execution—one path is taken if a condition is true, and the other path is taken if the condition is false.

E

element
An individual item in a list or tuple.

else suite
A block of statements that are part of a trystatement and appear after an elseclause. The block is executed after the try block is executed, but only if no exceptions were raised by the try block.

encapsulation
The combining of data and code into a single object.

End terminal End terminal

end user
The hypothetical person that is using a program.

end-line comment
A comment that appears at the end of a line of code.

ENIAC
The world’s first programmable electronic computer. ENIAC stands for Electronic Numerical Integrator and Computer.

Entry widget
A widget that allows the user to type text into.

error handler
Code that responds to an error.

error trap
Code that responds to an error.

escape character
A special character that is preceded with a backslash (\) appearing inside a string literal and treated as a special command.

escape sequences
A special character that is preceded with a backslash (\), appearing inside a string literal. Escape sequences are treated as special commands that are embedded in a string.



903
event handler
A function that executes when a specific event occurs.

event object
An object that contains information about an event.

event-driven
A type of program that waits for events to take place and responds to those events.

exception
An error that occurs and must be handled while a program is running.

exception
An error that occurs while a program is running.

exception handler
Code that responds to an exception and prevents the program from terminating.

exception object
An object that contains information about an exception and is created in memory when an exception is raised.

executing
The process of a computer performing the tasks that a program tells it to do.

external padding
Space that appears around the outside edge of a widget.

F

f-string
A string literal that contains special codes that cause the values of variables to be inserted and formatted.

fetch-decode-execute cycle
The three-step process that a CPU performs when executing each instruction in a program.

field
A single piece of data within a record.

field width designator
A code in an f-string that indicates the minimum width of a field.

filename extensions
Short sequences of characters that appear at the end of a filename preceded by a period.

file object
An object in memory that is associated with a file. The program uses the file object to work with the file.

finally suite
A block of statements that are part of a trystatement and appear after the finallyclause. The block is executed after the try block is executed and after any exception handlers have executed.

flag
A Boolean variable that signals when some condition exists in a program.

flash drives A USB drive

flash memory
A special type of memory used in storage devices such as USB drives.

floating-point notation
A technique for storing real numbers as binary values.

flowchart
A diagram that graphically depicts the steps that take place in a program.

foreign key
A column in one table that references a primary key in another table.


format specifier
A code in an f-string that causes a value to be formatted in a specific way.

Frame
A container that can hold widgets.

function
A body or prewritten code that performs an operation.

function
A group of statements that exist within a program for the purpose of performing a specific task.

function definition
Code that defines a function.

function header
The first line of a function definition.

G

garbage collection
A process that automatically removes data that is no longer referenced by a variable from memory.

global constant
A named constant that is available to every function in the program.

global variable
A variable that is defined outside all functions in a program. A global variable's scope is the entire program.

graphical user interface
(GUI) An interface that allows the user to interact with the operating system through graphical elements on the screen.

H

handler
The block of code that appears after an exceptclause in a try statement.





904   Glossary
hardware
The physical devices that a computer is made of.

hierarchy chart
A chart that shows the relationships between functions.

high-level languages
A programming language that uses words that are easier to understand than low-level instructions and allows you to create programs without knowing how the CPU works.

I

identity column
A column that contains unique values that are generated by the database management system.

index
An integer that specifies an element's position in a list.

infinite loop
A loop that has no way of stopping.

initializer method
A method that is automatically called when an object is created. It is typically used to initialize an object’s attributes with starting values.

Input
Any data the computer collects from people and from other devices.

input device
A component that collects input and sends it to the computer.

input file
A file that a program reads data from.

input symbol
A parallelogram symbol that represents an input operation.

input validation
The process of confirming the validity of input.

instance
An object that is created from a class.

instance attribute
An attribute that belongs to a specific instance of a class.

instruction set
The entire set of instructions that a CPU can execute.

integrated development environment
Specialized software that combines programming tools such as a text editor, a compiler or interpreter, a debugger, etc.

interactive mode
A mode in which the Python interpreter executes Python statements as you type them at the keyboard.

internal padding
Space that appears around the inside edge of a widget.

interpreter
A program that both translates and executes the instructions in a high-level language program.

intersection
The intersection of two sets is a set that contains only the elements that are found in both sets.

IPO chart
A chart that describes the input, processing, and output of a function.

iterable
An object that contains a sequence of values and can be iterated over with a loop.

iteration
A single execution of the body of a loop.


K

key
The part of a dictionary element that identifies the element. Dictionary keys are immutable.

key-value pairs
A data element that consists of two parts: a key and a value. To retrieve the value of the data element, you search for the key.

keyword argument
Function calling syntax that specifies which parameter, by name, that an argument is to be passed into.

keyword-only parameter
A parameter that accepts only keyword arguments.

keywords
A word that has a specific meaning in a programming language and cannot be used for any other purpose.

L

Label
A widget that displays text.

library functions
A collection of functions that are built into a programming language.

list
An object that can contain multiple data items.

list comprehension
An expression uses the contents of one list to produce another list.

Listbox
A widget that displays a list of items and allows the user to select one or more items from the list.

local variable
A variable that is created inside a function. Local variables cannot be accessed by statements outside the function in which they are created.



905
logic error
A mistake in a program that does not prevent the program from running but causes it to produce incorrect results.

logical operator
An operator that connects two Boolean expressions to create a compound Boolean expression.

loop
A control structure that repeats a statement or group of statements as many times as necessary.

low-level language
A programming language that is close in nature to machine language.

M

machine language
The instructions that are understood by a computer's CPU.

magic number
An unexplained number that appears in a program's code.

main memory
A computer's memory that stores programs while they are running, as well as the data the programs are working with.

mainline logic
The overall logic of a program.

many-to-one relationship Many rows in one table can
reference a single row in another table.

mappings
Another name for key-value pair.

math expression
Code that performs a calculation and gives a result.

math operator
A symbol that performs arithmetic on its operands.

memory sticks A USB drive

menu-driven program
A program that displays a list of choices for the user to choose from.

method
A function that belongs to an object and performs an operation using that object.

method
A function that works with an object's data.

microprocessors
A computer chip that acts as a central processing unit.

mixed-type expression
An expression that uses operands of different data types.

mnemonics
A short word that represents a machine language instruction.

modularization
Writing a program in such a way that each task is performed by a separate function.

modularized program
A program that has been written with each task performed by a separate function.

module
A Python source code file that can be imported into other Python programs

modulus operator
An operator that returns the remainder of a division

multiline string
A string literal that spans multiple lines in a program's code.

multiple assignment statement An assignment statement that
assigns values to multiple variables.

mutable
Able to be changed.

mutator method
A method that stores a value in a attribute or changes the value of a attribute in some other way.

N

named constant
A name that represents a value that cannot be changed during the program’s execution.

nested list
A list that contains other lists as its elements.

newline character
An invisible character that marks the end of a line of text.

newline escape character
An escape character that marks the location where a new line begins in the file.

numeric literal
A number that is written into a program’s code.

O

object
A software entity that contains both data and procedures.

object reusability
The ability to reuse an object for multiple purposes.

object-oriented programming
A programming paradigm that is centered on creating objects, which are software entities that contains both data and procedures.

one-to-many relationship
For each row in one table, there can be many rows in another table that reference it.





906   Glossary
operand
A value or piece of data that an operator uses to perform an operation.

operating system
The fundamental programs that control the internal operations of the computer’s hardware, manages all of the devices connected to the computer, allows data to be saved to and retrieved from storage devices, and allows other programs to run on the computer.

operators
A symbol or word that performs some operation on pieces of data.

Output
Data the computer produces for people or for other devices.

output device
A device that formats and presents output.

output file
A file that a program writes data to.

output symbol
A parallelogram symbol that represents an output operation.

overhead
Actions that take place in preparation for a function call, such as allocating memory for parameters and local variables, and storing the address of the return point.

override
When a subclass method has the same name as a superclass method.

P

padding
Space that appears around a widget.

parameter list
A list of two or more parameter variable declarations that appear in a function header.


parameter variable
A special variable that is declared in a function header and receives an argument that is passed into the function.

parameterized query
An SQL query that uses placehold-ers to insert the values of variables into the query.

pass by value
When an argument is passed by value, a copy of the argument is passed into the parameter variable.

passed by position
When the first argument is passed into the first parameter, the second argument is passed into the second parameter, and so forth.

pickle
To convert an object to stream of bytes that can be saved in a file.

picture element
A pixel, which is a tiny dot of color in an image.

pixel
A picture element, which is a tiny dot of color in an image.

pixel
A picture element, or tiny dot on the computer screen.

polymorphism
The ability of an object to take different forms.

positional-only parameter
A parameter that accepts only positional arguments.

precedence
The priority that an operator has when it is included in an expression that uses multiple operators.

precision designator
A code in an f-string that indicates the number of decimal places that a floating-point number should be rounded to.

pretest loop
A loop that tests its expression before each iteration.

primary key
A column that can be used to identify a specific row in a database table.

priming read
An input operation that occurs just before the first iteration of a loop that continues to read input.

private data attribute
An attribute that can be accessed only by code in the object's class.

private method
An object's method that can be accessed only by code inside the object's class.

problem domain
The set of real-world objects, parties, and major events related to a particular problem.

procedure
A function that performs a specific task.

processing symbol
A rectangle symbol that represents a process.

program
A set of instructions that a computer follows to perform a task

program development cycle
The process of designing, writing, correcting, testing, and debugging software.

programmer
A person with the training and skills necessary to design, create, and test computer programs.

prompt
A message that tells (or asks) the user to enter a specific value.



907
pseudocode
An informal language that has no syntax rules and is not meant to be compiled or executed.

pseudorandom number
A number that is seemingly random but is actually calculated.

public method
An object's method that can be accessed by code outside the object's class.

Python interpreter
A program that can read Python programming statements and execute them.

Python program
A file that contains Python statements.

Python script
A file that contains Python statements.

Python shell
The Python interpreter running in interactive mode.

Q

qualified column name
A name that identifies a column and the table to which the column belongs.

Quit button
A button that closes the program when the user clicks it.

R

radio button
A widget that can be either selected or deselected. Multiple radio buttons can be used together to let the user select one choice several possible options.

RAM
Random Access Memory

random access file
A file that allows the program to jump directly to a specific location within the file.

random-access memory
A type of memory that allows data to be quickly retrieved from random locations.

raw string
A string in which backslash characters are read as literal backslash characters, and not interpreted as escape sequences.

read position
The location of the next item that will be read from a file.

record
A complete set of data that describes one item.

recursive case
In a recursively solved problem, the case in which the problem must be reduced through recursion.

recursive function
A function that calls itself.

relational operator
An operator that compares two values and determines whether a specific relationship exists between them.

repetition operator
An operator that makes multiple copies of a sequence and joins them together.

repetition structure
A control structure that repeats a statement or group of statements as many times as necessary.

reserved words
A word that has a specific meaning in a programming language and cannot be used for any other purpose.

resource
An external object or data source that a program works with.

resource leak
A condition that can happen when a program fails to close all its open resources.

responsibilities
Things that a class is responsible for knowing and actions that a class is responsible for doing.

row
A complete set of data about a single item, stored in a database table.

running
The process of a computer performing the tasks that a program tells it to do.

running total
The ever-increasing total that is kept in an accumulator variable.

S

sample
A small piece of digital audio data.

scope
The part of a program in which a variable may be accessed.

screen coordinate system
A coordinate system that can be used to specify locations on the screen.

script mode
A mode in which the Python interpreter reads and executes the contents of a file that contains Python statements.

Secondary storage
A type of memory that can hold data for long periods of time, even when there is no power to the computer.





908   Glossary
seed value
A value that initializes the formula used to calculate pseudorandom numbers.

selection structure
A control structure that can execute a set of statements only under certain circumstances.

sentinel
A special value that signals the end of a series of values.

sequence
An object that contains multiple items of data, stored one after the other.

sequence structure
A set of statements that execute in the order in which they appear.

sequential access file
A file containing data that must be accessed in a sequential fashion, from the beginning of the file to the end of the file.

serialize
To convert an object to stream of bytes that can be saved in a file.

set
An object that stores a collection of unique, unordered elements.

set comprehension
A concise expression that reads the elements of one set and creates another set.

short-circuit evaluation Stopping the evaluation of a compound Boolean expression as
soon as the expression's value can be determined.

single alternative decision structure A decision structure that has only one alternative path of execution.

slice
A span of elements in a sequence.

software
Programs for a computer.

software developer
A person with the training and skills necessary to design, create, and test computer programs.

Software development tools Programs that programmers use to create, modify, and test software.

software requirement
A single function that a program must perform in order to satisfy the customer.

solid-state drive
A storage device that stores data in solid-state memory.

source code
The statements that a programmer writes in a programming language.

SQL injection
A security attack in which the user enters carefully crafted SQL code as input, causing that code to be inserted into the program's SQL code, causing a malicious action to be performed on the database.

standard library
A collection of prewritten functions that are included with a programming language.

Start terminal
The symbol that marks a program's beginning point in a flowchart.

state
The contents of an object's attributes at any given moment in time.

statement
A unit of code that consists of key words, operators, punctuation, and other allowable programming elements, arranged in the proper sequence to perform an operation.

step value
In a forloop, the amount by which the counter variable is incremented.

string
A sequence of characters.

string literal
A string that is written into a program's code.

structure chart
A chart that shows the relationships between functions.

Structured Query Language
A standard language for working with a database management system.

subclass
The specialized class in an inheritance relationship.

subset
A set of values that are also part of another set.

substring
A string that is taken from within a larger string.

superclass
The general class in an inheritance relationship.

superset
A set that contains a subset.

symmetric difference
The symmetric difference of two sets is the set of elements that are in one set but not in both.

syntax
A set of rules that must be strictly followed when writing a program.

syntax error
A mistake made by the programmer, such as a misspelled key word, a missing punctuation character, or the incorrect use of an operator.

system software
Programs that control and manage the basic operations of a computer.



909
T

target variable
The variable that is the target of an assignment during each iteration of a forloop.

terminal symbols
The oval symbols that designate the beginning and end of a flowchart.

text file
A file containing data that has been encoded as text, using a scheme such as ASCII or Unicode.

token
An item of data that is extracted from a string.

tokenizing
The process of breaking a string into tokens.

top-down design
A design process in which an algorithm is broken into functions.

traceback
An error message that gives information about the location in a program where an error occurred.

truncate
To throw away part of a piece of data, such as the fractional part of a number.

try suite
The block of code inside a try statement that can potentially raise an exception.

tuple
An immutable sequence.

turtle graphics
A graphics system that simulates a robotic "turtle" that draws lines and shapes as it moves about.

two-dimensional list
A list that contains other lists as its elements.

two’s complement
A technique for storing negative numbers as binary values.

type designator
A code in an f-string that indicates the type of value being displayed.

U

Unicode
An extensive character encoding scheme that is compatible with ASCII and can also represent the characters of many of the world’s languages.

USB drives
A Universal Serial Bus drive.

user
The hypothetical person that is using a program.

user interface
The part of a system that the user interacts with.

utility program
A program that performs a specialized task to enhance the computer’s operation or safeguards data.

V

value
The part of a dictionary element that holds a piece of data and is associated with a key. You retrieve the value by its key.

value-returning function
A function that returns a value back to the part of the program that called it.

variable
A name that represents a value in the computer’s memory.

vertex
The point where two line segments are connected.

void function
A function that simply executes the statements it contains and does not return a value back to the part of the program that called it.

volatile
A type of memory that is used for temporary storage while a program is running.

W

widget
The items that appear in a program’s graphical user interface.

wildcard character
A character that is used as a substituted for one or more characters in a string.

with suite
A block of code that is part of a withstatement and works with a resource.

4 Actually the Python interpreter is set up to look in various other predefined locations in your system when it does not find a module in the program’s folder. If you choose to learn about the advanced features of Python, you can learn how to specify where the interpreter looks for modules.

8 The parameter must be present in a method. You are not required to name it self, but this is strongly recommended to conform with standard practice.

9 In case you’re worried about the monks finishing their job and causing the world to end anytime soon, you can relax. If the monks move the discs at a rate of 1 per second, it will take them approximately 585 billion years to
move all 64 discs!
